title: Math Operands Should Be Cast Before Assignment in Java
description: In Java, when arithmetic operations are performed on integer types, the result is also an integer. If this integer result is assigned to a long, double, or float variable, automatic type conversion occurs. However, since the operation started as an integer, the result might not be as expected due to potential loss of precision or overflow. To avoid this, at least one operand should be cast or promoted to the final type before the operation takes place.
recommendation: To fix this issue, you should cast or promote at least one of the operands to the final type before the arithmetic operation. This ensures that the result will be of the expected type and no precision is lost or overflow occurs.
impact: Not casting or promoting operands before arithmetic operations can lead to unexpected results due to loss of precision or overflow, potentially leading to bugs or vulnerabilities in the code.

checks.message: To fix this issue, you should cast or promote at least one of the operands to the final type before the arithmetic operation. This ensures that the result will be of the expected type and no precision is lost or overflow occurs.
